value.market_read: Headline: US crude drew 3.8 mb to 408.4 mb as runs held firm at 96.6% utilization (+0.5 pts, 17.2 mb/d crude inputs), while gasoline drew 2.3 mb to 214.0 mb heading into peak driving season. Distillate built 2.5 mb to 108.6 mb and jet added 1.7 mb — the clean-barrel split tells the story of a market pulling gasoline hard while diesel and jet lag. Driver: Cushing ticked up 0.71 mb to 19.67 mb but remains firmly below the ~25 mb operational floor — the tank complex has been sub-floor for weeks (22.4 mb on May 29 down to 18.96 mb on June 19), and this week's uptick does little to relieve deliverability stress. The gasoline draw lines up with firm 8.96 mb/d 4-week demand (up from 8.84 mb/d prior), a proper draw on strengthening summer pull. Trade flows: Crude exports averaged 4.46 mb/d over 4 weeks, down sharply from 5.35 mb/d, while imports eased to 5.47 mb/d from 5.88 mb/d — a net loosening that partly explains why the headline crude draw wasn't deeper despite peak-season runs. PADD III drove the crude draw with a 2.3 mb pull as Gulf Coast utilization pushed to 98.1%. Watch next week: Whether Cushing's marginal build extends or reverses back toward the mid-18s — sub-floor tanks keep prompt WTI backwardation supported. Also watch if gasoline draws accelerate on the 8.96 mb/d demand trajectory against a shrinking 214 mb inventory just as hurricane season risk builds on the Gulf Coast.

padd.padd1: PADD I gasoline drew 0.77 mb as East Coast supply tightened alongside the national 2.3 mb draw. Refinery utilization collapsed 8.2 points to just 68.3%, a steep cut that leaves the region leaning heavily on Colonial Pipeline arrivals and waterborne imports to cover peak-season gasoline demand. Distillate built 1.6 mb — the region's largest product move — while jet drew 0.43 mb. With NYH RBOB structure the swing factor, backwardation would pull barrels north via Colonial and rebuild the gasoline draw, while contango keeps nominations light and supply lean. Watch: Colonial nominations and NYH RBOB cash diffs — a firm prompt premium is needed to offset the collapsed 68.3% run rate feeding peak demand.
padd.padd2: Cushing built 0.71 mb to 19.67 mb but remains well below the ~25 mb operational floor, where it has sat for weeks after touching 18.96 mb on June 19. This week's modest tick-up barely dents the sub-floor deliverability stress that keeps prompt WTI backwardation firmly bid. PADD II crude stocks rose 0.79 mb overall and utilization held near capacity at 99.8% (-0.2 pts), with gasoline up 0.2 mb and distillate up 0.6 mb. Watch: Whether Cushing extends this build or slides back toward the mid-18s — every draw below floor deepens tank-bottom risk and steepens the prompt WTI curve.
padd.padd3: PADD III crude drew 2.3 mb, driving the national crude draw, as Gulf Coast utilization climbed 1.4 points to 98.1% near peak. Gasoline drew 2.2 mb — the region's dominant product move — while jet built 1.7 mb and distillate was flat at +0.06 mb. The steep 4-week export slide to 4.46 mb/d from 5.35 mb/d suggests softer Asian buying pace is leaving more barrels on the coast even as runs stay elevated. Watch: Hurricane season risk now live since June 1 against 98.1% utilization — any storm-driven outage would strand crude and spike product cracks, while a rebound in Asian buying would tighten LOOP sour differentials.
padd.padd4: PADD IV utilization surged 6.6 points to 103.2%, signaling a return from turnaround as Rockies refiners ran flat-out. Crude drew 0.41 mb and distillate eased 0.15 mb, while gasoline built a modest 0.16 mb and jet drew 0.12 mb. The above-100% run rate points to strong Bakken and DJ basin crude uptake feeding the regional system. Watch: Whether the 103.2% rate holds or fades — sustained peak runs will draw regional crude and could firm Bakken differentials into midsummer.
padd.padd5: PADD V crude drew 1.25 mb as West Coast utilization eased 0.5 points to 93.7%. Jet led product moves with a 0.89 mb build and distillate added 0.37 mb, while gasoline built a modest 0.26 mb against the CARB-spec isolated market. The crude draw against slightly softer runs suggests leaner waterborne supply into the LA Basin. Watch: LA Basin gasoline margins and the pace of Phillips 66 Rodeo conversion effects on regional supply — CARB isolation keeps the West Coast vulnerable to any single-refinery hiccup.
